description: Capability-selected managed runtime using only live cloud core surfaces and integration-tested local control-plane services.

bindings.failureBoundary: Key custody is an operator-supplied local AES key rather than managed HSM/KMS custody.
Publication drains the outbox without external delivery; consumers must read the durable API ledger.
Evidence availability and ranking depend on the deployment PostgreSQL instance.
Memory availability shares the runtime PostgreSQL failure domain.

The retained live proof covers one activated preset-model chat invoked with a 32-token request ceiling; the provider reported 48 output tokens, so the claim is request forwarding rather than provider-side ceiling enforcement.
limitations: provider usage may exceed the requested max_tokens value
no throughput or regional failover claim

bindings.failureBoundary: Presigned URLs are bearer credentials, cannot be revoked by the runtime, and must never enter checkpoints or evidence.
GET/HEAD are bounded; absence is 404, while other provider failures reject with sanitized metadata.
PUT uses bounded attempts and payload size; bucket policy, versioning, and conditional writes are outside the claim.
The private CPU sandbox is bounded to the retained lifecycle and fixed file/command limits; Terminating is not terminal cleanup.
limitations: no VPC, mounts, GPU, or warm-capacity claim

bindings.failureBoundary: Only in-process registered toolsets are reachable; provider recovery policy remains mandatory.
Only skills registered in the current process are resolvable.
limitations: no remote registry or cross-process distribution
